creator: makc@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Pavel Timofeev from comment #8)
> Many committers ask to run these tools. Do we have inconsistency?
> Or it is to make review easier, and run those at the last step by committer?

Indeed the use of port linters is a good practice, you definitely want to use it for your ports. However, there are some nuances. Firstly the style changes should be separated from the functional changes and software updates in general. Secondly, avoid restyling team maintained ports as they may have different set of formatting rules. I can't speak for gnome@ ports, but it is the case for kde@ ones.
